source_jobs.title: QA Lead
source_jobs.description:
About Foundation Health

Foundation Health is transforming healthcare through an AI-powered digital pharmacy platform that seamlessly connects operational infrastructure with high quality patient experiences. Our mission is to transform patient centric care by connecting fragmented infrastructure, optimizing care coordination, and removing friction from the patient journey. We refuse to adhere to the status quo; instead, we actively pioneer solutions that will shape the healthcare practices of tomorrow.



This ambitious vision is only achievable with the dedication of the right team propelling us forward. We firmly believe that a supportive and inspiring work environment fuels creativity, transforming it into groundbreaking innovation. It is this very innovation that not only benefits our organization but also positively impacts our people, partners, and most importantly, our patients.



At Foundation Health, we foster a culture that encourages our team members to broaden their horizons, urging them to bring their passion and curiosity to the workplace each day. We understand that diverse perspectives fuel progress, and we actively seek individuals who share our commitment to excellence and forward-thinking.



The Mission: Quality-First Healthcare
Healthcare is plagued by outdated, frustrating workflows. At Foundation Health, we believe that thoughtfully applied AI can finally simplify these systems. We are revolutionizing the pharmacy space by building deep, integrated infrastructure that changes how care is delivered.

As our QA Lead, you aren’t just managing a process from afar - you are the hands-on guardian of an AI-powered platform that directly impacts provider workdays and patient lives. This is a hybrid role based in our Orlando, FL hub, where you will work side-by-side with our VP of Engineering (ex-DeepMind/Google) to architect and execute a modern quality strategy at the absolute intersection of health-tech and artificial intelligence.

The Role: Active Leadership & Deep Technical Grit
We are an AI-native team. We don’t just build AI; we use it to maximize our output and automate the mundane. We are not looking for a hands-off manager. We need a proactive, technically sharp Lead who is deeply familiar with the nuances of healthcare data and thrives on the unique challenge of testing non-deterministic, AI-powered systems.

In this role, you will:

 - Lead from the Trenches: Actively own and execute manual, exploratory, and automated testing alongside your team. You scale quality by doing, coaching, and setting the technical standard.

 - Tame AI Complexity: Design testing strategies that account for LLM behaviors, prompt validations, and model hallucinations, ensuring our AI products are safe and reliable for clinical use.

 - Protect the Patient Experience: Act as the final point of hands-on accountability for release readiness. You aren't afraid to "put your foot down" if a product isn't ready for primetime.

 - Master the Fundamentals: From deep-dive regression testing to owning complex UAT implementations with external healthcare clients, you ensure our move-fast culture doesn't break mission-critical workflows.


Our Tech & Your Toolkit
We’ve cleared the path with a modern, serverless stack designed for speed: Node.js, TypeScript, SvelteKit, and GCP.

 - Automation: Master, maintain, and scale our Playwright frameworks for front-end testing.

 - APIs: Use tools like Postman to ensure our core integration platforms meet strict quality standards.

 - The AI Frontier: Get hands-on with Playwright MCP and Agents, leveraging cutting-edge AI tools to test complex features in production.


What You Bring (The Blueprint)
This is a specialized role requiring a unique blend of industry compliance knowledge and cutting-edge tech fluency.


Core Requirements:

 - Healthcare Domain Expertise: 2+ years of experience testing within the healthcare, health-tech, or pharmacy space. You understand HIPAA considerations, data pr

source_jobs.title: Principal Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Foundation Health

Foundation Health is transforming healthcare through an AI-powered digital pharmacy platform that seamlessly connects operational infrastructure with high quality patient experiences. Our mission is to transform patient centric care by connecting fragmented infrastructure, optimizing care coordination, and removing friction from the patient journey. We refuse to adhere to the status quo; instead, we actively pioneer solutions that will shape the healthcare practices of tomorrow.



This ambitious vision is only achievable with the dedication of the right team propelling us forward. We firmly believe that a supportive and inspiring work environment fuels creativity, transforming it into groundbreaking innovation. It is this very innovation that not only benefits our organization but also positively impacts our people, partners, and most importantly, our patients.



At Foundation Health, we foster a culture that encourages our team members to broaden their horizons, urging them to bring their passion and curiosity to the workplace each day. We understand that diverse perspectives fuel progress, and we actively seek individuals who share our commitment to excellence and forward-thinking.


Fixing Healthcare, One System at a Time

Healthcare runs on workflows built in the 90s. We're changing that - specifically in pharmacy, where we're building AI-powered infrastructure that plugs directly into Electronic Health Records and helps health systems operate at scale. Not a chatbot. Real, deep infrastructure that changes how care gets delivered.



We're Series A, already live with enterprise-level customers. The product works, people want it - now we're in building mode.



The Role

This is the most senior individual contributor position in our new Orlando hub - and it's a big one.



You'll be working directly alongside our VP of Engineering (ex-DeepMind/Google) as the technical anchor for the hub. That means owning architecture across multiple product areas, setting the standards others build to, and making the kind of calls that shape where this platform is in two years - not just this quarter.



Crucially - this isn't a purely strategic role. We need someone who can lead system design and get into the codebase when it matters. At Foundation Health, technical credibility comes from doing, not just directing.



And you're doing all of this on a completely greenfield codebase. No legacy applications, no decade-old technical debt, no red tape slowing you down. Just hard, interesting problems and the autonomy to solve them properly.



What you'll be doing

You'll own system design at platform level - architecting for 10-100x growth without major rewrites, establishing the patterns and standards the wider engineering org adopts, and identifying root-cause issues before they become expensive problems.



On the AI side, you'll define how we use it - not just in the product, but across the engineering organisation. Overseeing the architecture of AI-powered features (voice agents, clinical workflows), managing model selection, latency and cost tradeoffs, evaluation methodologies, and setting the quality and safety bar for everything AI-related that ships.



You'll work closely with product leadership to shape the roadmap - pushing back where needed, identifying what's high-leverage versus what's noise, and driving cross-team initiatives where success is measured by actual product outcomes, not ticket throughput.



On the people side, you'll grow the Engineers around you and help build the next generation of technical leaders in the hub.



What we're looking for

 - A track record of building complex systems from scratch - and being able to get back into the code when needed, not just review it

 - Strong engineering judgment - you know when to move fast and when to slow down, and you frame technical decisions in terms of business outcomes

 - Genuine AI fluency - experience building AI-powered products and u

source_jobs.title: Implementation Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Foundation Health

Foundation Health is transforming healthcare through an AI-powered digital pharmacy platform that seamlessly connects operational infrastructure with high quality patient experiences. Our mission is to transform patient centric care by connecting fragmented infrastructure, optimizing care coordination, and removing friction from the patient journey. We refuse to adhere to the status quo; instead, we actively pioneer solutions that will shape the healthcare practices of tomorrow.



This ambitious vision is only achievable with the dedication of the right team propelling us forward. We firmly believe that a supportive and inspiring work environment fuels creativity, transforming it into groundbreaking innovation. It is this very innovation that not only benefits our organization but also positively impacts our people, partners, and most importantly, our patients.



At Foundation Health, we foster a culture that encourages our team members to broaden their horizons, urging them to bring their passion and curiosity to the workplace each day. We understand that diverse perspectives fuel progress, and we actively seek individuals who share our commitment to excellence and forward-thinking.

ABOUT THE ROLE

As an Implementation Manager, you will drive the end-to-end delivery of customer implementations of our AI-powered digital pharmacy platform. You'll work cross-functionally with Sales, Engineering, Product, and Business Operations to deliver solutions that are on time, on scope, and genuinely impactful for our health system customers.



This role is for someone who thrives in the controlled chaos of an early-stage company — someone who brings structure to ambiguity, builds trust with customers quickly, and gets things done. You'll be the primary point of contact for your customers, the connective tissue between internal teams, and the person who makes sure nothing falls through the cracks.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

Implementation Execution & Delivery

 - Lead customer implementations from kickoff through go-live, owning the plan, the timeline, and the outcome

 - Execute against established implementation methodology and playbooks, contributing improvements as you learn what works

 - Create and manage project plans, status reports, risk/issue logs, and other implementation collateral with precision and follow-through

 - Coordinate internal resources and external partners to ensure smooth, on-time delivery

 - Proactively surface risks, issues, and dependencies - escalate when needed, and own the resolution

Customer Relationship Management

 - Serve as the primary point of contact for your customers throughout the implementation lifecycle

 - Build strong, trust-based relationships with clinical, operational, and IT stakeholders at health system customers

 - Deliver clear, consistent implementation updates to internal and external stakeholders on a regular cadence

 - Help maximize NPS, customer satisfaction, and revenue recognition through best-in-class delivery

Cross-Functional Collaboration

 - Partner with Sales on scoping, deal transitions, and setting accurate customer expectations pre-implementation

 - Collaborate with Product and Engineering to surface customer feedback, flag integration challenges, and inform the roadmap

 - Contribute to the development of implementation playbooks, templates, and best practices that improve repeatability across the team



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

Required:

 - 3+ years of customer-facing project or program management experience in enterprise healthcare SaaS or health technology

 - Experience at an early-stage, venture-backed healthcare technology company (Seed through Series B) - you know what it means to thrive in a startup environment

 - Proven success managing end-to-end implementations of enterprise healthcare software, SaaS, or AI solutions at U.S. health systems

 - Experience launching new enterprise healthcare technology products and building the deliv

source_jobs.title: Head of Finance
source_jobs.description:
About Foundation Health

Foundation Health is transforming healthcare through an AI-powered digital pharmacy platform that seamlessly connects operational infrastructure with high quality patient experiences. Our mission is to transform patient centric care by connecting fragmented infrastructure, optimizing care coordination, and removing friction from the patient journey. We refuse to adhere to the status quo; instead, we actively pioneer solutions that will shape the healthcare practices of tomorrow.



This ambitious vision is only achievable with the dedication of the right team propelling us forward. We firmly believe that a supportive and inspiring work environment fuels creativity, transforming it into groundbreaking innovation. It is this very innovation that not only benefits our organization but also positively impacts our people, partners, and most importantly, our patients.



At Foundation Health, we foster a culture that encourages our team members to broaden their horizons, urging them to bring their passion and curiosity to the workplace each day. We understand that diverse perspectives fuel progress, and we actively seek individuals who share our commitment to excellence and forward-thinking.




THE ROLE

In a high-growth environment, finance isn't just about accounting; it's about resource orchestration. As our Head of Finance, you will sit at the intersection of strategy and execution. Your journey begins by translating our capital and momentum into strategic growth, ensuring our resources are deployed with precision.

In the early days, you will be a builder. You’ll own and scale the entire finance function from the ground up, implementing the financial infrastructure and "cutting-edge" tools required for a high-growth US startup. You will shift us from foundational setups to high-fidelity reporting, giving our leadership team crystal-clear visibility into revenue, burn, and margins.

As we scale, your role shifts from builder to architect. You will become the primary strategic partner to our CEO, modeling our next big moves, evaluating new market opportunities, and leading the charge on future fundraises. You won’t just be reporting on the past; you’ll be the person architecting our financial future.



THE PATH AHEAD

This role is designed for a high-trajectory leader who has played a key role in the scaling phase of a successful startup and is now ready to own the entire function.



WHAT YOU’LL OWN

While this is a strategic leadership role, the foundations must be rock solid. You will be responsible for:

 - Strategic Planning: Lead financial forecasting, budgeting, and long-term planning (runway, burn, and growth scenarios) while developing robust sales and revenue models.

 - Operational Excellence: Oversee day-to-day accounting operations, including AP, AR, payroll, and cash flow management.

 - Financial Reporting: Own monthly board packs and financial reporting, translating data into actionable recommendations for the leadership team.

 - Partner with the CEO in board and investor interactions, providing clear financial narrative and insight

 - Infrastructure & Compliance: Evolve our finance tech stack by selecting and implementing modern, scalable systems. You will also manage US federal and state tax obligations in coordination with external vendors.

 - Performance Tracking: Build visibility into core financial metrics, including unit economics, margins, and department-level budgeting.

 - Team Growth: Build and scale the finance function over time, including hiring and developing a high-performing team.”

 - Risk Management and Controls: Establish financial controls, risk management frameworks, and audit readiness as the company scales.

 - Treasury & Capital Efficiency: Drive capital allocation decisions to balance growth, efficiency, and runway.

 - Strategic Finance: Support strategic initiatives such as partnerships, acquisitions, or new market entry through financial evaluation.



THE

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Foundation Health

Foundation Health is transforming healthcare through an AI-powered digital pharmacy platform that seamlessly connects operational infrastructure with high quality patient experiences. Our mission is to transform patient centric care by connecting fragmented infrastructure, optimizing care coordination, and removing friction from the patient journey. We refuse to adhere to the status quo; instead, we actively pioneer solutions that will shape the healthcare practices of tomorrow.



This ambitious vision is only achievable with the dedication of the right team propelling us forward. We firmly believe that a supportive and inspiring work environment fuels creativity, transforming it into groundbreaking innovation. It is this very innovation that not only benefits our organization but also positively impacts our people, partners, and most importantly, our patients.



At Foundation Health, we foster a culture that encourages our team members to broaden their horizons, urging them to bring their passion and curiosity to the workplace each day. We understand that diverse perspectives fuel progress, and we actively seek individuals who share our commitment to excellence and forward-thinking.



Fixing Healthcare, One System at a Time

Healthcare runs on workflows built in the 90s. We're changing that — specifically in pharmacy, where we're building AI-powered infrastructure that plugs directly into EHR platforms, helping health systems actually function at scale. Not a chatbot. Not a wrapper. Real, deep infrastructure that changes how care gets delivered.



We're a Series A company already in production with enterprise customers already - so, the PMF question is answered. Now we're building the team that scales it.



The Role

This is a mid-level role for someone who's past the hand-holding stage but still has plenty of runway to grow. You'll own features end-to-end, work on a modern stack (Node/TypeScript/GCP), and be surrounded by senior engineers worth learning from.



You don't need to have worked in our exact stack. If you're a strong engineer who loves building things and picks up new tools quickly, that matters more to us than ticking a language checkbox.



We're AI-native — Cursor, Claude — and we expect you to use these tools as a natural part of how you work, not as a novelty.



What we're looking for

 - A couple of years of professional experience building and shipping software in a production environment

 - Solid fundamentals — you understand what you're building and why it works

 - Someone who asks good questions, takes feedback well, and gets things done without needing constant direction

 - Already experimenting with AI tools in your workflow

 - Strong educational background in CS or similar

 

Why now, why us

You'd be joining as one of the founding engineers in a new hub. That means real influence — on the product, the architecture, and the culture we build here. Meaningful equity, a seat at the table, and work that actually touches people's lives through the healthcare system.

source_jobs.title: Pharmacy Operations Specialist (PharmD)
source_jobs.description:
About Foundation Health

Foundation Health is transforming healthcare through an AI-powered digital pharmacy platform that seamlessly connects operational infrastructure with high quality patient experiences. Our mission is to transform patient centric care by connecting fragmented infrastructure, optimizing care coordination, and removing friction from the patient journey. We refuse to adhere to the status quo; instead, we actively pioneer solutions that will shape the healthcare practices of tomorrow.



This ambitious vision is only achievable with the dedication of the right team propelling us forward. We firmly believe that a supportive and inspiring work environment fuels creativity, transforming it into groundbreaking innovation. It is this very innovation that not only benefits our organization but also positively impacts our people, partners, and most importantly, our patients.



At Foundation Health, we foster a culture that encourages our team members to broaden their horizons, urging them to bring their passion and curiosity to the workplace each day. We understand that diverse perspectives fuel progress, and we actively seek individuals who share our commitment to excellence and forward-thinking.

ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Pharmacy Operations Specialist (PharmD), you will support health system customers through every stage of their journey with our AI-powered digital pharmacy platform - from kickoff and go-live through long-term adoption and growth. You'll bring deep pharmacy and pharmacy operations expertise throughout the implementation process, helping customers configure the platform to optimize their workflows and training their teams with confidence. Once customers are live, you'll shift into a customer success motion helping drive adoption and identifying opportunities to maximize value/ROI for our health system customers.

This role is ideal for a PharmD who has worked for a health system pharmacy or at a healthcare technology company supporting health system customers. You'll combine clinical and operational credibility with a structured, consultative approach across the entire customer journey.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

Implementation Support

 - Support implementations as the clinical and operational subject matter expert from contract signature through go-live and stabilization

 - Facilitate discovery sessions with health system pharmacy teams to understand workflows, system configurations, and operational requirements, translating these into a tailored implementation plan

 - Configure and validate the platform against customer-specific pharmacy workflows, formulary structures, and operational protocols

 - Help deliver training to pharmacist and technician end-users, ensuring teams are confident and competent ahead of go-live

 - Participate in customer onsites, virtual meetings, and calls

 - Help prepare customer-facing materials including training guides, onboarding documentation, and go-live checklists

 - Serve as the voice of the customer internally, channeling implementation feedback and insights to the Product team to inform the roadmap

Post-Go-Live Support

 - Support the ongoing customer relationship after go-live, particularly with peer pharmacists, pharmacy technicians, and/or health system leadership

 - Partner with Customer Success to maximize adoption and usage, proactively identifying customers who may need additional support, training, or re-engagement

 - Support regular business reviews with health system pharmacy leadership to assess value realization, surface challenges, and align on goals

 - Drive ongoing training and enablement initiatives to ensure teams are fully utilizing platform capabilities

 - Help identify and pursue expansion opportunities - including additional use cases, user growth, and new departmental adoption - in partnership with the Sales, Customer Success, and Account Management teams

 - Serve as the voice of the customer internally, channeling post-go-live feedback

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Foundation Health

Foundation Health is transforming healthcare through an AI-powered digital pharmacy platform that seamlessly connects operational infrastructure with high quality patient experiences. Our mission is to transform patient centric care by connecting fragmented infrastructure, optimizing care coordination, and removing friction from the patient journey. We refuse to adhere to the status quo; instead, we actively pioneer solutions that will shape the healthcare practices of tomorrow.



This ambitious vision is only achievable with the dedication of the right team propelling us forward. We firmly believe that a supportive and inspiring work environment fuels creativity, transforming it into groundbreaking innovation. It is this very innovation that not only benefits our organization but also positively impacts our people, partners, and most importantly, our patients.



At Foundation Health, we foster a culture that encourages our team members to broaden their horizons, urging them to bring their passion and curiosity to the workplace each day. We understand that diverse perspectives fuel progress, and we actively seek individuals who share our commitment to excellence and forward-thinking.



The Mission

Foundation Health is building the future of healthcare delivery, and we are looking for a high-agency Senior Product Manager to lead the charge. This isn't a role for someone focused on maintaining a single feature set; you will own our entire product ecosystem, spanning patient-facing applications, complex clinician workflows, AI-driven automation, and data-heavy system integrations. We are early in our journey, offering you a rare, genuine opportunity to shape our product direction, influence core strategy, and leave a meaningful imprint on the foundation of what we build.



The Impact You’ll Make

 - Architect the Future: Own the end-to-end product idealization and execution across multiple interconnected systems and workflows.

 - Solve Complex Challenges: Translate complex clinical, operational, and technical requirements into clear, scalable product specifications.

 - Lead AI & Automation: Help shape AI-driven and automation-led capabilities specifically designed to improve efficiency and patient outcomes.

 - Drive Integration: Work deeply with EHR data, clinical workflows, and interoperability challenges. Map upstream and downstream dependencies across healthcare systems and third-party integrations.

 - Partner for Execution: Partner closely with Design and Engineering to craft the end-to-end user journey, define solution architecture, and build truly scalable integrations.

 - Move with Rigor: Balance high-speed delivery with technical rigor in a fast-moving, high-growth startup environment.



The Profile We’re Looking For

 - The Builder Mindset: You have a proven track record of building products from 0 to 1, not just supporting them. You enjoy getting into the technical details—not just writing tickets—and you view yourself as a product builder.

 - Experience & Education: You have 4+ years of experience as a Product Manager building software products in a technology-driven environment. You possess a strong healthcare technology background, with direct experience working in clinical or operational healthcare systems.

 - Technical Depth: You have hands-on experience with Electronic Health Records (EHRs) and exposure to products operating at the intersection of EHRs, complex clinical systems, data, and AI. You have direct experience working with clinical data, workflows, or healthcare integrations.

 - Startup DNA: You have demonstrated success in a startup or high-growth environment, with an inherent ability to move fast, operate in ambiguity, and get stuck in.

 - A Sharp Operator: You are a sharp, curious PM who enjoys solving the hardest problems. You are comfortable navigating technical, clinical, and operational domains simultaneously, and you are known for challenging assumptions to

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Foundation Health

Foundation Health is transforming healthcare through an AI-powered digital pharmacy platform that seamlessly connects operational infrastructure with high quality patient experiences. Our mission is to transform patient centric care by connecting fragmented infrastructure, optimizing care coordination, and removing friction from the patient journey. We refuse to adhere to the status quo; instead, we actively pioneer solutions that will shape the healthcare practices of tomorrow.



This ambitious vision is only achievable with the dedication of the right team propelling us forward. We firmly believe that a supportive and inspiring work environment fuels creativity, transforming it into groundbreaking innovation. It is this very innovation that not only benefits our organization but also positively impacts our people, partners, and most importantly, our patients.



At Foundation Health, we foster a culture that encourages our team members to broaden their horizons, urging them to bring their passion and curiosity to the workplace each day. We understand that diverse perspectives fuel progress, and we actively seek individuals who share our commitment to excellence and forward-thinking.



Fixing Healthcare, One System at a Time



Healthcare runs on workflows built in the 90s. We're changing that — specifically in pharmacy, where we're building AI-powered infrastructure that plugs directly into EHR platforms, helping health systems actually function at scale. Not a chatbot. Not a wrapper. Real, deep infrastructure that changes how care gets delivered.



We're a Series A company already in production with enterprise customers already - so, the PMF question is answered. Now we're building the team that scales it.



The Role

Senior Engineer at Foundation Health means hands-on, high-ownership work. You'll contribute to system design, take on complex features end-to-end, and care about the quality of what ships — not just whether your ticket is closed.

 

You'll work closely with Staff engineers and directly shape how we build. This isn't a "execute the spec" role.

 

What you'll be doing

Writing clean, well-considered code on a modern Node/TypeScript/GCP stack — no legacy baggage. Picking up hard problems, producing solid design docs, and reviewing your peers' work in a way that actually helps them improve.



We're not precious about your current stack. A strong engineer can pick up a new language almost immediately — we care far more about how you think and what you've built.



You'll use AI tools as a natural part of how you work — Cursor, Claude — and contribute to features that incorporate AI directly. We're native by design, not bolting it on.



You'll flag issues early, push back when something doesn't make sense, and take real ownership over the surface area you work on — correctness, reliability, the lot.



What we're looking for

 - Solid experience building production systems — you can point to things you've shipped and talk through the decisions

 - High agency — you don't need perfect information to move forward

 - Comfortable with some ambiguity; Series A pace is real

 - Already using AI tools in your day-to-day

 - Strong CS fundamentals

 

Why now, why us

You'd be joining as one of the founding engineers in a new hub. That means real influence — on the product, the architecture, and the culture we build here. Meaningful equity, a seat at the table, and work that actually touches people's lives through the healthcare system.

source_jobs.title: QA Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Foundation Health

Foundation Health is transforming healthcare through an AI-powered digital pharmacy platform that seamlessly connects operational infrastructure with high quality patient experiences. Our mission is to transform patient centric care by connecting fragmented infrastructure, optimizing care coordination, and removing friction from the patient journey. We refuse to adhere to the status quo; instead, we actively pioneer solutions that will shape the healthcare practices of tomorrow.



This ambitious vision is only achievable with the dedication of the right team propelling us forward. We firmly believe that a supportive and inspiring work environment fuels creativity, transforming it into groundbreaking innovation. It is this very innovation that not only benefits our organization but also positively impacts our people, partners, and most importantly, our patients.



At Foundation Health, we foster a culture that encourages our team members to broaden their horizons, urging them to bring their passion and curiosity to the workplace each day. We understand that diverse perspectives fuel progress, and we actively seek individuals who share our commitment to excellence and forward-thinking.



Fixing Healthcare, One System at a Time

Healthcare runs on workflows built in the 90s. We're changing that - specifically in pharmacy, where we're building AI-powered infrastructure that plugs directly into EHRs and helps health systems operate at scale. Not a chatbot. Real, deep infrastructure that changes how care gets delivered.



We're Series A, already live with enterprise-level customers, and now building the team that scales it.



The Role

QA at Foundation Health isn't a checkbox exercise. You're the person who decides whether something is ready to ship - and in healthcare, that actually matters.



We're looking for someone who can do both things well: the rigour of proper QA - regression testing, UAT, documentation, knowing when to dig in by hand - and the newer stuff. Testing AI-powered systems, using AI tooling to supercharge your own output, getting hands-on with Playwright MCP and agentic workflows before most QA engineers have even heard of them.



If you're the kind of person who takes genuine pride in the quality of what ships, and isn't afraid to push back when something isn't ready — this is the role.



What you'll be doing

You'll be shaping our QA culture from scratch - frameworks, standards, the lot. Greenfield, no legacy debt to wrestle with.



On the technical side: Playwright for automated front-end testing, Postman for API testing across our core platforms, and a modern serverless stack in Node.js, TypeScript, SvelteKit and GCP. You'll also get early hands-on time with Playwright MCP and AI agents as we ship real features into production - this is genuinely new territory for most teams.



You'll work closely with engineers to catch issues early, support complex implementations, and make sure our move-fast culture doesn't compromise the product. When something isn't ready, you'll say so - and people will listen.



What we're looking for

 - 3+ years in QA or software testing, with solid experience on web applications and APIs

 - Strong Playwright skills and a good instinct for when to automate and when to test by hand

 - Experience working on enterprise-level products - you understand what's at stake when things go wrong

 - Already using AI tools in your workflow, or hands-on experience testing AI-powered products

 - Someone who cares about quality as an outcome, not just a process



The setup

We're based in Manchester city centre and that's our preference for this role. That said, if you're London-based and the rest of this resonates, we're open to a conversation - we have a presence there too.



We're not prescriptive about office days, but we do think there's something in getting in a room together to whiteboard, work through hard problems, and actually build a team. We're lo

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Foundation Health

Foundation Health is transforming healthcare through an AI-powered digital pharmacy platform that seamlessly connects operational infrastructure with high quality patient experiences. Our mission is to transform patient centric care by connecting fragmented infrastructure, optimizing care coordination, and removing friction from the patient journey. We refuse to adhere to the status quo; instead, we actively pioneer solutions that will shape the healthcare practices of tomorrow.



This ambitious vision is only achievable with the dedication of the right team propelling us forward. We firmly believe that a supportive and inspiring work environment fuels creativity, transforming it into groundbreaking innovation. It is this very innovation that not only benefits our organization but also positively impacts our people, partners, and most importantly, our patients.



At Foundation Health, we foster a culture that encourages our team members to broaden their horizons, urging them to bring their passion and curiosity to the workplace each day. We understand that diverse perspectives fuel progress, and we actively seek individuals who share our commitment to excellence and forward-thinking.




THE MISSION: FIXING HEALTHCARE WITH CODE



Healthcare is full of complex, frustrating workflows that haven’t changed in decades. We believe that thoughtfully applied AI can finally simplify these systems, improving both the provider's workday and the patient's experience.



Specifically, we are revolutionizing the pharmacy space. We’re building AI-powered systems that integrate directly with Electronic Health Record (EHR) platforms, helping health systems operate effectively at scale. This isn't a "chatbot on a landing page" - it’s deep, integrated infrastructure that changes how care is delivered.




THE TEAM: AI-NATIVE BY DESIGN

We’re led by a technical leadership team with deep roots in high-scale engineering, including our VP of Engineering (ex-DeepMind/Google). Together, we’re fostering an "AI-native" culture where we don’t just build AI; we use it.



We want engineers who lean on AI tools to maximize their output and automate the mundane parts of their day-to-day work. We’re creating an environment where using the latest tools isn't just allowed - it’s encouraged and championed by leadership.




LOCATION & CULTURE

We have an office in Manchester City Centre, and while we aren't a "mandatory X days in the office" company, we believe in the power of getting together. We’re looking for engineers who are excited to head into Manchester when needed to whiteboard, jam on ideas, and build rapport in person.




WHY YOU’D LEAVE YOUR CURRENT ROLE

 - The AI Playground: You’ll get hands-on with OpenAI, Langfuse, and Vertex AI to ship real-world features. We aren't just "researching" AI; we are shipping it into production environments where it actually impacts patient care.

 - True Feature Ownership: You won’t just be handed well-scoped tickets. You will own full features end-to-end, driving them from initial ambiguity and architectural design all the way to production.

 - Influence the Technical Direction: As an early senior hire, you will have a direct seat at the table. You will collaborate closely with our VP of Engineering to shape our technical stack, establish engineering standards, and influence the product roadmap.

 - Pure Product Engineering: We are looking for people who thrive when the path isn't fully paved. If you love the feeling of architecting a feature, shipping it, and seeing it in a customer's hands 48 hours later, you’ll fit in.

 - Significant Equity: Because you’re joining in this early window as a senior pillar of the team, you’ll take a meaningful stake in what we are building.




WHAT WE DO CARE ABOUT

 - You’re an Owner and Builder: You have a proven track record of shipping production-grade applications from scratch. You can talk deeply about system design choices, trade-offs, and ho

We firmly believe that a supportive and inspiring work environment fuels creativity, transforming it into groundbreaking innovation. It is this very innovation that not only benefits our organization but also positively impacts our people, partners, and most importantly, our patients.



At Foundation Health, we foster a culture that encourages our team members to broaden their horizons, urging them to bring their passion and curiosity to the workplace each day. We understand that diverse perspectives fuel progress, and we actively seek individuals who share our commitment to excellence and forward-thinking.



Fixing Healthcare, One System at a Time

Healthcare runs on workflows built in the 90s. We're changing that — specifically in pharmacy, where we're building AI-powered infrastructure that plugs directly into Electronic Health Record platforms, helping health systems actually function at scale. Not a chatbot. Not a wrapper. Real, deep infrastructure that changes how care gets delivered.



We're a Series A company already in production with enterprise customers already - so, the PMF question is answered. Now we're building the team that scales it.



The Role

We're building out our engineering hub in Orlando, and we want a Staff Engineer who's genuinely excited about owning something — not just contributing to it. You'll be a technical anchor for one of our core products, making architectural calls that matter six months from now, not just this sprint.



This is a hands-on role. You'll write code, not just review it.



What you'll be doing

You'll set the technical bar — through your own code as much as your decisions. You'll design systems that span multiple components, produce design docs the broader team actually uses, and spot the debt hotspots before they become fires.



On the AI side: we're native by design. Cursor, Claude, the lot. You'll design agentic workflows (automated reviews, LLM-assisted QA) and contribute directly to AI-powered features — RAG, function calling, evaluation frameworks. You'll also help pick the tooling and set the patterns others follow.



Beyond the code, you'll own delivery across workstreams, manage cross-team dependencies, and translate between what engineering can do and what the business needs. You'll grow the people around you through coaching and hiring, not through process overhead.



What we're looking for

 - A track record of building complex systems from scratch — and being able to talk about the specific decisions you made and why

 - High agency. You don't wait for a perfectly scoped ticket. You figure out what needs to happen and do it.

 - Comfortable in ambiguity. We're a Series A — things move fast and change often

 - Already using AI tools daily, and interested in pushing that further

 - Strong educational foundation in CS or a related field



The stack: Node/TypeScript/GCP. No legacy code. Greenfield all the way.



Why now, why us

You'd be joining as one of the founding engineers in a new hub. That means real influence — on the product, the architecture, and the culture we build here. Meaningful equity, a seat at the table, and work that actually touches people's lives through the healthcare system.

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Foundation Health

Foundation Health is transforming healthcare through an AI-powered digital pharmacy platform that seamlessly connects operational infrastructure with high quality patient experiences. Our mission is to transform patient centric care by connecting fragmented infrastructure, optimizing care coordination, and removing friction from the patient journey. We refuse to adhere to the status quo; instead, we actively pioneer solutions that will shape the healthcare practices of tomorrow.



This ambitious vision is only achievable with the dedication of the right team propelling us forward. We firmly believe that a supportive and inspiring work environment fuels creativity, transforming it into groundbreaking innovation. It is this very innovation that not only benefits our organization but also positively impacts our people, partners, and most importantly, our patients.



At Foundation Health, we foster a culture that encourages our team members to broaden their horizons, urging them to bring their passion and curiosity to the workplace each day. We understand that diverse perspectives fuel progress, and we actively seek individuals who share our commitment to excellence and forward-thinking.



The Mission: Fixing Healthcare with Code

Healthcare is full of complex, frustrating workflows that haven’t changed in decades. We believe that thoughtfully applied AI can finally simplify these systems, improving both the provider's workday and the patient's experience.



Specifically, we are revolutionizing the pharmacy space. We’re building AI-powered systems that integrate directly with Electronic Health Record (EHR) platforms, helping health systems operate effectively at scale. This isn't a "chatbot on a landing page" - it’s deep, integrated infrastructure that changes how care is delivered.



The Team: AI-Native by Design

We’re led by a technical leadership team with deep roots in high-scale engineering, including our new VP of Engineering (ex-DeepMind/Google). Together, we’re fostering an "AI-native" culture where we don’t just build AI; we use it.



We want engineers who lean on AI tools to maximize their output and automate the mundane parts of their day-to-day work. We’re creating an environment where using the latest tools isn't just allowed - it’s encouraged and championed by leadership.



Location & Culture:

We have an office in Manchester City Centre, and while we aren't a "mandatory X days in the office" company, we believe in the power of getting together. We’re looking for engineers who are excited to head into Manchester when needed to whiteboard, jam on ideas, and build rapport in person.



That said, we are a distributed team. If you’re a high-performer that's not local, you aren't an afterthought - you’re a first-class citizen in this mission.



Why you’d leave your current role:

 - The AI Playground: You’ll get hands-on with OpenAI, Langfuse, and Vertex AI to ship real-world features. We aren't just "researching" AI; we are shipping it into production environments where it actually impacts patient care.

 - High Agency, High Ownership: You will have the autonomy to own features from ideation to production. You’ll have a direct seat at the table to influence the product roadmap and the technical direction of the company.

 - No Legacy Debt: You won’t be spending 40% of your week fixing 10-year-old bugs. You’ll be writing new code on a modern Node/TS/GCP stack.

 - Pure Product Engineering: We are looking for people who thrive when the path isn't fully paved. If you love the feeling of shipping a feature and seeing it in a customer's hands 48 hours later, you’ll fit in.

 - Significant Equity: Because you’re joining in this early window, you’ll take a meaningful stake in what we are building.



What we do care about:

 - You’re a Builder: You can show us things you’ve built from scratch and talk about your specific contributions toward those products.

 - You’re a Problem Solver: You don

notes: High tech role ratio, frequent postings, and lack of sponsorship info suggest a startup with a strong engineering focus.
